situation: Deep audit audit-2026-07-02-003 found HIGH severity H-001: packages/afw/src/core/config.ts updateConfig() used raw writeFile instead of atomicWrite, risking config.json corruption on crash/concurrent write. Every other sensitive store (secrets.ts, access-keys.ts, masking.ts) correctly uses atomicWrite. Also lacked serialized read-modify-write chain, allowing concurrent patch clobbers.
action: Replaced writeFile with atomicWrite (from ./atomic-file.ts). Added configWriteChain serialized promise chain mirroring mutateSecrets pattern in secrets.ts. Removed now-unused mkdir/dirname imports (atomicWrite handles mkdir internally). API signature unchanged — all 8 callers transparent.
outcome_evidence: Commit landed in opt-agentfw-1782961299. Diff: -writeFile import +atomicWrite import; -mkdir/dirname imports; updateConfig body replaced with configWriteChain.then() wrapping atomicWrite call. API signature (patch: Partial<AfwConfig>) → Promise<AfwConfig> preserved. Push to remote failed (403 read-only mirror) — non-blocking per opt-worktree.sh design.
next_suggestion: Dispatch M-001 fix (token lookup linear scan → in-memory Map index). Then M-002 (CORS origin:* + bind address), M-003 (ReDoS protection), L-001 (pattern dedup). After all 5 route-fixes merged/rejected, audit-cycle-state transitions to cycle-complete → triggers next deep audit.

audit_findings.finding: updateConfig used raw writeFile instead of atomicWrite — crash/concurrent write can corrupt config.json
audit_findings.remediation: Replace with atomicWrite + add serialized write chain
